name: Reflex (Full-Stack Web Apps in Pure Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Reflex Dev. Cloud hosting available at reflex.dev

auth.notes: No auth built-in; implement via state management

performance.notes: Next.js frontend + FastAPI backend; WebSocket state sync; SSR for initial load

agent_readiness.notes: Full-stack web apps in pure Python — compiles to React/Next.js. import reflex as rx. class State(rx.State): count: int = 0. def increment(self): self.count += 1. def index(): return rx.button('Click', on_click=State.increment). app = rx.App(). app.add_page(index). rx.run() for dev server. Components: rx.button, rx.input, rx.table, rx.chart. Database: built-in with SQLModel. Auth: rx.AuthState. Deploy: rxconfig.py for deployment config. Transpiles Python to TypeScript React via pynecone compiler. No JavaScript required. Hot reload in dev.

gotchas: Transpilation adds complexity — Python semantics don't map 1:1 to React
WebSocket state sync means all state changes go through Python backend
Still maturing — API changes between versions, breaking changes possible
Performance overhead from WebSocket round-trips for every state change
Complex state management can be difficult to debug across Python/JS boundary
best_when: Python-only teams building full-stack CRUD apps without JavaScript expertise
Internal tools needing more UI sophistication than Streamlit provides
Rapid prototyping of Python-backed web applications
When deploying to Reflex cloud hosting
avoid_when: High-performance web applications (WebSocket overhead per interaction)
Complex frontend interactions better handled in JavaScript/TypeScript
summary: Reflex builds full-stack web apps in pure Python, compiling to React/Next.js. class State(rx.State): count: int = 0. Components: rx.button(on_click=State.increment). WebSocket state sync between Python backend and React frontend. Built-in SQLModel database, auth, and deployment. Apache-2.0. No JavaScript required. Still maturing — best for Python teams needing more web sophistication than Streamlit without learning JavaScript.
